Nx identifies flaky tasks by tracking the outcomes of your tasks in relation to your source code and external dependencies. When the same code produces different results, Nx flags the task as flaky and triggers the warning."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Detecting flaky tasks in the local environment empowers developers to address issues early in the development cycle—before they affect your CI pipelines."}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"project-detail-view-enhancements","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Project Detail View Enhancements",["$","$La",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#project-detail-view-enhancements","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["As part of ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/concepts/inferred-tasks","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"project Crystal","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," we've added the ability to our plugins to automatically infer tasks based on the tools and configuration files available in your workspace. ",["$","code",null,{"children":"targetDefaults"}]],["$","$La",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#pattern-support-for-targetdefaults","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/ci/features/split-e2e-tasks","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Atomizer features","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," of plugins like ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/playwright","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/playwright"}],"target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," and ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/cypress","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/cypress"}],"target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", you end up creating tasks with dynamic but predictable names."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2024-08-01/e2e-task-names.avif","alt":"Tasks Created With Atomizer","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Notice how the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"e2e-ci--src/example.spec.ts"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"e2e-ci--src/test.spec.ts"}]," tasks here are created by the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/playwright"}]," plugin dynamically based on the playwright spec files present in your workspace."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["We've now ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"added pattern matching"}]," to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"targetDefaults"}]," task names as a way to target these dynamic tasks."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"In the past it was difficult to customize this dynamic set of targets. But with the new support for specifying a pattern to a target name, you can now customize this set of dynamic targets all at once."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The following will set all such tasks to depend on their ",["$","code",null,{"children":"build"}]," task:"]}],["$","$Lc",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"children":"{\n  \"targetDefaults\": {\n    \"e2e-ci--**/*\": {\n      \"dependsOn\": [\"build\"]\n    }\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Note that our plugins will set sensible configurations here out of the box when creating new workspaces using our generators."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Read more about ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/recipes/running-tasks/reduce-repetitive-configuration#reduce-configuration-with-targetdefaults","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":["reducing configuration with ",["$","code",null,{"children":"targetDefaults"}]],"target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," and ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/features/run-tasks#defining-a-task-pipeline","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":["how you can define task pipelines with ",["$","code",null,{"children":"targetDefaults"}]],"target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"individual-targets-can-now-opt-out-of-parallelism","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Individual Targets Can Now Opt Out of Parallelism",["$","$La",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#individual-targets-can-now-opt-out-of-parallelism","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["One of our goals in transforming the CI landscape is to ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"make CI more declarative"}]," by focusing on defining the \"what\" rather than the \"how.\""]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"To accommodate port collisions in end-to-end tests, we've long adjusted our generated CI script to look like this:"}],["$","$Lc",null,{"language":"yml","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"children":"- run: npx nx affected -t lint test build\n- run: npx nx affected --parallel 1 -t e2e-ci\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Unfortunately, this approach is more imperative, as it instructs how to run and order your CI rather than defining what should be run. Additionally, these instructions cause ",["$","code",null,{"children":"lint"}],", ",["$","code",null,{"children":"test"}],", and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"build"}]," targets to run first and wait until they all complete before running ",["$","code",null,{"children":"e2e-ci"}],", leading to inefficiencies."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["To address this, all tasks now support a ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/reference/project-configuration#parallelism","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":[["$","code",null,{"children":"parallelism"}]," property"],"target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],". By setting this property to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"false"}],", you can instruct the Nx task runner not to run a specific task in parallel. This allows us to define parallelism as a task property, making our CI configuration more declarative."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Both our ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/playwright"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/cypress"}]," plugins will now automatically set ",["$","code",null,{"children":"targetDefaults"}]," for atomized tests to disable parallelism:"]}],["$","$Lc",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"nx.json","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"children":"{\n  // ...\n  \"targetDefaults\": {\n    \"e2e-**/*\": {\n      // ...\n      \"parallelism\": false\n    }\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"This way we can simply run the command:"}],["$","$Lc",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"children":"nx affected --targets=lint,test,build,e2e-ci\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"In CI, Nx Agents will allow all tasks to run in parallel on the same machine, except for the atomized end-to-end tasks, which will only run in isolation."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Learn more about how you can ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/ci/intro/tutorials/github-actions#parallelize-tasks-across-multiple-machines-using-nx-agents","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Parallelize Tasks Across Multiple Machines Using Nx Agents","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],":"]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"text-center","children":[" ",["$","iframe",null,{"src":"https://www.youtube.com/embed/0YxcxIR7QU0","title":"Faster e2e Tests!","width":"100%","allow":"accelerometer; Incremental builds in Nx workspaces allow you to build any package in your workspace individually, and will then automatically use that built artifact when then building any project or application that consumes the package. This way you can speed up your build and CI times through optimizations like ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/nx/documents/affected","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"only building packages that were affected","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", using ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/ci/features/remote-cache","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx Replay","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," to effortlessly cache and share your built artifacts, and allowing you to run builds of your various packages in parallel using ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/ci/features/distribute-task-execution","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/concepts/buildable-and-publishable-libraries","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"buildable libraries and incremental ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/concepts/inferred-tasks","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx Project Crystal","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," in Nx version 18, we've adjusted our generators to leverage these features when creating new workspaces and projects. We've now added the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"convert-to-inferred"}]," generator to give you an automated way to opt into project crystal."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"To run the generator, you can run the command:"}],["$","$Lc",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"children":"npx nx g convert-to-inferred\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The important thing to note here is that while this generator will make adjustments to the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project.json"}]," file of the projects you convert, the behavior of your tasks should remain the same. You can see project details via the ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/getting-started/editor-setup","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx Console","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," plugin of your IDE, or in a browser by running the command: ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx show project [projectName]"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["In Nx 19, we were focused on adding ",["$","code",null,{"children":"convert-to-inferred"}]," to our ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/playwright","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Playwright","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/cypress","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Cypress","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", and ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/eslint","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"ESLint","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," plugins - where bringing the ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/ci/features/split-e2e-tasks","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx Atomizer","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," provided the greatest value. Since then, we've added this generator to most other plugins. Currently, our React native plugins (react native, expo, detox) are the only plugins that are outstanding, and we'll be providing ",["$","code",null,{"children":"convert-to-inferred"}]," generators here in the near future."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"gradle-composite-builds-support","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Gradle Composite Builds Support",["$","$La",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#gradle-composite-builds-support","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["In ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/blog/nx-19-release","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx 19.0","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", we introduced support for Gradle through our ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/gradle","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Gradle plugin","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],". With the latest update, this plugin now also supports ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/composite_builds.html","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Gradle Composite builds","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", in addition to multi-project builds."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Gradle Composite builds are commonly used in larger workspaces to manage dependencies across multiple projects. With this new support, Nx can now leverage composite builds to enhance the Nx Task Graph. For example, consider the following ",["$","code",null,{"children":"settings.gradle.kts"}]," file:"]}],["$","$Lc",null,{"language":"kotlin","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"children":"rootProject.name = \"app\"\n\nincludeBuild(\"number-utils\")\nincludeBuild(\"string-utils\")\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Nx will now be able to automatically determine the dependencies between the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"app"}]," project and the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"number-utils"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"string-utils"}]," workspaces:"]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2024-08-01/gradle-composite-dep-graph.avif","alt":"Gradle Composite Project Dep Graph","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["You can read more on composite builds in the ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/composite_builds.html","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Gradle documentation","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"experimental-gradle-test-atomization","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Experimental: Gradle Test Atomization",["$","$La",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#experimental-gradle-test-atomization","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The new ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/gradle","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":[["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/gradle"}]," plugin"],"target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," now includes experimental support for ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/ci/features/split-e2e-tasks","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Test Atomization","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2024-08-01/gradle-atomized.avif","alt":"Gradle Atomized","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["With Test Atomization, as you add tests to your Gradle projects, Nx will automatically create a separate task for each test class. This allows you to distribute the execution of these tasks across multiple machines on CI, similar to the functionality provided by our ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/playwright"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/cypress"}]," plugins."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"experimental-nx-release-adds-file-based-versioning-support","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Experimental: Nx Release Adds File Based Versioning Support",["$","$La",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#experimental-nx-release-adds-file-based-versioning-support","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Currently with ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/nx/documents/release","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Nx release","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", you already have two different ways of figuring out the how to modify the current version of a package to get its updated version:"]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":["Imperatively via the CLI/prompt by telling it your desired relative (semver keyword) or absolute (e.g. ",["$","code",null,{"children":"1.2.3"}],") version."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Declaratively by letting Nx inspect your git history and determine the semver bump to apply based on the conventional commits specification."}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Nx release now supports an experimental third way of deriving the version bump that we call \"version plans\"."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"The idea behind version plans is that you want to track your intended version bumps alongside your changes, just like you would do with conventional commits, but you want to make this tracking independent from your actual commit data. This might be because you do not want to/cannot use the conventional commits standard in your workspace, or it could just be that you find it a cleaner approach to versioning."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Version plan files use frontmatter enhanced markdown for their contents, and can be created by hand or by leveraging the new ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx release plan"}]," subcommand. This will guide you through creating a git-tracked file within ",["$","code",null,{"children":".nx/version-plans"}]," that will be committed alongside your normal changes."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2024-08-01/nx-release-plan-command.avif","alt":"Nx Release Plan Command","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["When your team is ready to release, you can now simply run the CLI command ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx release"}]," (or use the ",["$","$La",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/features/manage-releases#using-the-programmatic-api-for-nx-release","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"powerful programmatic API","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],") and nx will ingest all the outstanding version plan files and use the combined information to determine the resulting version for all referenced projects and/or release groups as part of the versioning, changelog generation, and publishing steps. (You can also run the command ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx release version"}]," if all you want to do is update the version!)"]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2024-08-01/nx-release-with-plans.avif","alt":"Nx Release With Plans","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Anyone who has used the tools \"Changesets\" or \"Beachball\" will be familiar with the concept of file based versioning, but with Nx release you get the power to combine one or more of the above mentioned versioning strategies all within the same workspace for different release groups, and we are really excited to keep building out this feature set and mark it as non-experimental (by fully documenting it) very soon.
